properties.description: Delta file to CSV export to Container.

source:
# ============================================================
# Cell 1 — Configuration
# ============================================================

storage_account = "navispowerbidatastorage2"       # e.g. "mictadls"
container       = "mundra-ct03"             # e.g. "mictdata"
output_file     = "CT03_ecevents_6Months_28052026.csv"

base_path    = f"abfss://{container}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net"
delta_path   = f"{base_path}/silver/ecevents"
staging_path = f"{base_path}/export/ecevents_staging/"    # Temp folder for coalesce output
final_path   = f"{base_path}/export/"     # Final destination

# ============================================================
# Cell 2 — Read Delta Table & Apply Filters
# ============================================================

df = spark.read.format("delta").load(delta_path)

df_filtered = df.filter(
    
    ((df["Year"] == 2026) | (df["Year"] == 2025) & (df["month"] >= 12))
)

print(f"Total records: {df_filtered.count()}")
df_filtered.printSchema()

# ============================================================
# Cell 3 — Write as Single CSV to Staging Folder
# ============================================================

df_filtered.coalesce(1).write \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("header", "true") \
    .csv(staging_path)

print("✅ Written to staging folder.")

# ============================================================
# Cell 4 — Rename the Part File to a Clean CSV Name
# ============================================================

from notebookutils import mssparkutils

# Find the part-*.csv file in the staging folder
files = mssparkutils.fs.ls(staging_path)
part_file = [f.path for f in files if f.name.startswith("part-") and f.name.endswith(".csv")][0]

# Move & rename to final destination
dest = f"{final_path}{output_file}"
mssparkutils.fs.cp(part_file, dest, recurse=False)

# Clean up staging folder
mssparkutils.fs.rm(staging_path, recurse=True)

print(f"✅ Final CSV saved to: {dest}")

# ============================================================
# Cell 5 — Verify Output
# ============================================================

df_verify = spark.read.option("header", "true").csv(dest)

print(f"Row count  : {df_verify.count()}")
print(f"Column count: {len(df_verify.columns)}")
df_verify.show(5)
